project.name: Ghost Build Tracker
project.current_focus: TASK-010: merge unmerged branches (TASK-006 circuit-breaker, TASK-007 sub-workflow interfaces, 7D-ii ghost_events) + implement Ghost_Classify logical boundary

core_framing.title: Core framing / governing principles
principles: Ghost is a general orchestrator and governed capability platform, not a hardcoded assistant with fixed roles.
One pinned conversation owner stays responsible per thread.
Workers are used only through explicit delegation.
Every delegation becomes a visible task/work item.
Capabilities are preferred over raw tools.
Shared context and source-of-truth discipline outrank flashy autonomy.
MVP target is the end of Phase 7.
Phase 8 is post-MVP and does not replace MVP closure.
The canonical 10-phase spine remains stable while subphases stay flexible.
Home Assistant remains the control boundary for the Home domain.
openclaw_alignment: One pinned conversation owner per thread.
Workers are only invoked through explicit delegation.
No silent model/provider switching inside a single user conversation.
Every delegation creates a visible task/work item.
Worker sessions remain separate from the parent conversation.

phases.title: Phase 1 — Foundation Lab
phases.summary: Foundation lab baseline remains established across compute/runtime benching, early service bring-up, and first toy workflow loops.

steps.title: 1A Compute and runtime bench
steps.detail: Initial local compute/runtime benching patterns are established and reusable.

steps.title: 1B Core services bootstrapping
steps.detail: Core services bootstrapping flow is established for repeatable local bring-up.

steps.title: 1C Initial model strategy
steps.detail: Initial model strategy direction was established for early routing and fallback behavior.

steps.title: 1D Repo and workspace discipline
steps.detail: Repository and workspace discipline conventions are in place for operator-safe execution.

steps.title: 1E First end-to-end toy workflow
steps.detail: First end-to-end toy workflow path was proven to validate core control flow.

phases.title: Phase 2 — Core System of Record and Service Topology
phases.summary: Phase 2 remains established, while 2E schema migration strategy is explicitly tracked as an active load-bearing architectural lane.

steps.detail: Authoritative architecture shape for core services and execution flow is established.

steps.title: 2B Database split and persistence model
steps.detail: Database split and persistence model patterns are largely established.

steps.title: 2C Environment and deployment layout
steps.detail: Environment and deployment layout conventions are established for controlled operations.

steps.title: 2D Reliability groundwork
steps.detail: Reliability groundwork for baseline operations is largely in place.

steps.title: 2E Schema Migration Strategy
steps.detail: Migration sequencing, safety envelopes, and reversibility contracts are tracked as active architectural pressure without claiming implementation completion.

phases.title: Phase 3 — Orchestration Core and Runtime Discipline
phases.summary: Phase 3 is substantially complete, but v3.0 reopens major architectural load-bearing work around async delegation, circuit breaking, runtime decomposition, source extraction, and Redis activation.

steps.title: 3A Conversation ownership model
steps.detail: Pinned conversation ownership model is established as a core orchestration rule.

steps.title: 3B Explicit delegation model
steps.detail: Explicit delegation model remains active and is now expanded for asynchronous delegation pathways.

substeps.title: 3B-ii Asynchronous Delegation via Message Bus
substeps.detail: Message-bus delegation contracts are tracked as active architecture work and are not marked complete.

steps.title: 3C Worker identity and routing
steps.detail: Worker identity and routing contracts are established, with v3.0 hardening focused on failure control paths.

substeps.title: 3C-ii Circuit Breaker for Model Routing
substeps.detail: Circuit-breaker behavior across providers is tracked as active and load-bearing, without completion claims.

steps.title: 3D Tool boundary discipline
steps.detail: Tool-boundary discipline remains active under governed execution constraints.

steps.title: 3E Runtime Decomposition
steps.detail: Runtime decomposition into sub-workflows is an active v3.0 architecture lane and part of immediate pressure.

steps.title: 4E Memory contracts for workers

phases.title: Phase 7 — Governed Execution, Approvals, Followthrough, MVP Finish
phases.summary: Phase 7 is the active delivery zone and now explicitly carries write-ahead followthrough, rollback/undo, event sourcing, notification integration, and multi-user schema preparation.

steps.title: 7A Delegation visibility
steps.detail: Delegation visibility remains a first-class operational requirement.

steps.title: 7B Approval system
steps.detail: Approval-system behavior remains active with decision and unblock flow hardening.

steps.title: 7C Governed followthrough
steps.detail: Governed followthrough remains active, with v3.0 reliability sub-lanes kept explicit.

substeps.title: 7C-ii Write-Ahead Pattern
substeps.detail: Write-ahead followthrough pattern is tracked as active architecture-sensitive work.

substeps.title: 7C-iii Rollback / Undo Capability
substeps.detail: Rollback/undo capability across code, DB, and artifacts is tracked as active and not yet closed.

steps.title: 7D Audit trails
steps.detail: Audit-trail continuity remains active as the operator trust backbone.

substeps.title: 7D-ii Event Sourcing Layer
substeps.detail: Event-sourcing layer centered on ghost_events is tracked as active architecture work.

steps.title: 7E Baseline guardrails
steps.detail: Baseline guardrails remain active and continue tightening under governance constraints.

steps.title: 7F Pre-MVP observability baseline
steps.detail: Pre-MVP observability baseline remains active with operator-facing signal quality improvements.

steps.title: 7G MVP v3.0 Gate (17-item)
steps.detail: v3.0 MVP gate criteria are tracked explicitly. Gate note: schema migration system must be in place, and runtime business logic must live in version-controlled source files with unit tests.

substeps.title: Gate 01 — Ghost Main owns conversations and delegates through governed path
substeps.detail: Conversation ownership and governed delegation are required for MVP closure.

substeps.title: Gate 02 — Forge, Probe, Scout complete real delegated work
substeps.detail: At least three workers must complete real delegated tasks through visible task lanes.

substeps.title: Gate 03 — Runtime decomposed into 5 sub-workflows with testable source files
substeps.detail: Runtime decomposition and source extraction are required and currently tracked as active architecture work.

substeps.title: Gate 04 — Redis active as delegation queue, event bus, and capability cache
substeps.detail: Redis activation is required and remains an active architecture lane.

substeps.title: Gate 05 — Circuit breaker active on all model providers
substeps.detail: Circuit-breaker behavior must be present across providers and remains active work.

substeps.title: Gate 06 — Tasks visible in Task Board with accurate state transitions
substeps.detail: Task Board state transitions must reflect real delegated lifecycle truth.

substeps.title: Gate 07 — Structured memory active and conversation search operational
substeps.detail: Structured memory is active; conversation search operationalization remains tracked as active expansion work.

substeps.title: Gate 08 — Operator surfaces function with real data including cost analytics
substeps.detail: Operator surfaces must show real runtime data and cost analytics rather than placeholders.

substeps.title: Gate 09 — Approval system blocks destructive actions and records decisions
substeps.detail: Approval controls must enforce destructive-action blocking with durable decision records.

substeps.title: Gate 10 — Followthrough executor completes full loop with write-ahead pattern
substeps.detail: Followthrough executor + write-ahead loop closure remains active architecture-sensitive work.

substeps.title: Gate 11 — Rollback capability available for code/DB/artifact reversal
substeps.detail: Rollback/undo across code, database, and artifact layers remains active and not yet closed.

substeps.title: Gate 12 — ghost_events event sourcing is the audit backbone
substeps.detail: ghost_events-backed event sourcing must anchor audit truth and remains active work.

substeps.title: Gate 13 — Guardrails interface defined with baseline rules
substeps.detail: Guardrails interface and baseline governance rules must be concretely defined.

substeps.title: Gate 14 — Notification system delivers UI + at least one push channel
substeps.detail: Notification center plus at least one push channel are required for gate closure.

substeps.title: Gate 15 — Multi-user schema columns present but not activated
substeps.detail: Schema preparation for multi-user must be present without enabling full activation yet.

substeps.title: Gate 16 — Onboarding flow exists with real interactions
substeps.detail: Onboarding flow must be implemented with real user interactions.

substeps.title: Gate 17 — Governance architecture review documented
substeps.detail: Governance architecture review outcomes must be documented and reviewable.

steps.title: 7H Notification Integration
steps.detail: Notification integration across runtime and operator surfaces is tracked as active v3.0 delivery scope.

steps.title: 7I Multi-User Schema Preparation
steps.detail: Multi-user schema preparation is active without claiming production activation.
